qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Remote admin path prefix authorization bypass exposes sibling config paths
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets `admin.go` path-prefix authorization on the remote admin API, while the advisory targets swallowed CA-loading errors in `modules/caddytls/connpolicy.go` that make mTLS client authentication fail open.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in this session identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a fail-open mTLS bug in `ClientAuthentication.provision()` caused by swallowed CA-loading errors; the only qualifying finding is a separate remote-admin authorization-prefix bug.
evidence: The advisory JSON and upstream fix both identify `modules/caddytls/connpolicy.go` where `ClientAuthentication.provision()` returned `nil` instead of propagating errors from `convertPEMFilesToDER(...)` and `caPool.Provision(ctx)`, allowing mTLS to fall back to the system trust store.

The only final `qualifying_vuln` finding in the session is `remote_admin_path_prefix_authorization_bypass`, which reports `strings.HasPrefix`-based path authorization on the remote admin API and demonstrates an unauthorized read of `/config/.../tenant10` using a scoped client certificate.
That finding's report and PoC do not involve `TrustedCACertPEMFiles`, CA-file parsing, `ClientAuthentication.provision()`, `cfg.ClientCAs`, or any fail-open client-certificate verification behavior.
